rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports belong in component type scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope rules when inserting AGREE contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the input model.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope for AGREE expressions.
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should appear after subcomponents and connections in implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL properties like Period or Priority as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE expressions
Boolean data range comparisons in AGREE contracts
Event port numeric encoding in AGREE
